question: The Java client opens the subscription above for jobs of type `serviceA`. How does the docs say the client retrieves jobs from the workflow engine under the hood?

options.text: Long polling — the subscription uses long polling internally to retrieve jobs.
The engine pushes jobs to the worker over a persistent WebSocket from the broker.
The client listens on an internal JMS queue exposed by the Zeebe broker.
Jobs are written to a shared filesystem volume that the worker polls every second.

a.text: The docs state that subscriptions are how a handler is connected to Zeebe and that they internally use long polling to retrieve jobs.

b.text: The transport is gRPC/REST with long polling on subscriptions, not a WebSocket push pattern from the broker.

c.text: Zeebe does not expose a JMS queue for clients; subscriptions are the documented mechanism.

d.text: Shared filesystem polling is not part of the Zeebe client model — jobs are activated through the subscription API.

explanation: `newWorker().jobType(...).handler(...).open()` registers a subscription that pulls activated jobs from Zeebe using long polling. The handler is then invoked per job — typically completing the job via `client.newCompleteCommand(job.getKey()).send()` (or letting the Spring `@JobWorker` autoComplete do it).

docs.title: Connecting the workflow engine with your world
